نظر شما درباره‌ی سلطنت مصر و اینکه اطراف آن را به من بسپاری و زمام اختیار آن را در کف من بنهی، چیست؟ اکنون رأی خود را به ما بگو. من امور مصریان را از آن روی سامان ندادم که آن را ویران کنم و آنان را از بلا نجات ندادم تا خود، بلای جان آنان شوم، این پروردگار متعال بود که آنان را به دست من نجات داد». پادشاه گفت: «هرطوری که خودت می‌خواهی، رفتار کن. رأی، رأی توست». یوسف (علیه السلام) گفت: «پادشاها! خداوند و شما را به گواهی می‌گیرم که من همه‌ی مردم مصر را از بردگی، آزاد کرده و اموال و بردگان‌شان را به آن‌ها پس دادم و ای پادشاه به شما نیز انگشتر و تاج و تخت را باز پس دادم، به این شرط که روش مرا در حکومت اتّخاذ کرده و جز با مشورت من، حکم نفرمایی». پادشاه به یوس-ف (علیه السلام) گفت: «برای من، بسی مایه‌ی مباهات و افتخار خواهد بود که به راهی جز راه تو نروم و جز به فرمان و مشورت تو حکمرانی نکنم؛ اگر تو نبودی، مرا قدرت انجام چنین اقداماتی نبود و هیچ‌گاه به چنین سمت‌وسویی رهنمون نمی‌شدم. تو حکومت مرا چنان عزیز و پایدار ساختی که کسی را یارای دستیابی به آن نیست. من گواهی می‌دهم که هیچ معبودی، جز الله نیست، یکی است و شریکی ندارد و تو رسول خدایی؛ پس در اجرای مسئولیّتی که به تو داده‌ام، بکوش که تو نزد ما جایگاهی والا داری».
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۹۲
entries.references: البرهان
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Rida (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Yusuf/ 57)

English translation: Husayn ibn Ali ibn Bint Ilyas said: I heard al-Rida (peace be upon him) saying: "Yusuf (peace be upon him) turned his attention to gathering food, and during the seven fertile years, he collected it and stored it in the granaries. When those years passed and the years of famine arrived, Yusuf (peace be upon him) began selling the food. In the first year, he sold it for dirhams and dinars, until not a single dinar or dirham remained in Egypt and its surroundings that did not become the property of Yusuf (peace be upon him). In the second year, he sold it for jewelry and precious stones, until no jewelry or precious stones remained in Egypt and its surroundings that did not become his property. In the third year, he sold it for livestock and cattle, until no beast or cattle remained in Egypt and its surroundings that did not become his property. In the fourth year, he sold it for male and female slaves, until no slave or bondmaid remained in Egypt and its surroundings that did not become his property. In the fifth year, he sold it for houses and real estate, until no house or property remained in Egypt and its surroundings that did not become his property. In the sixth year, he sold it for farms and water sources, until no river or farm remained in Egypt and its surroundings that did not become his property. In the seventh year, he sold it for their own persons, until no slave or free person remained in Egypt and its surroundings who did not become a servant to Yusuf (peace be upon him). Thus, he came to own their free people, their slaves, and their wealth. The people said: 'We have never seen or heard of a king to whom God has given such rule, wisdom, knowledge, and management as He has given this king.' Then Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to the King: 'O King, what is your view regarding the dominion over Egypt and its surroundings that my Lord has entrusted to me? Is it, in your opinion, a source of harm for us? For I did not reform them only to corrupt them, nor did I save them from the calamity only to become a calamity upon them; rather, it was God Almighty who saved them through my hands.' The King said: 'The decision is yours.' Yusuf (peace be upon him) said: 'I call God to witness, and I call you to witness, O King, that I have emancipated all the people of Egypt, and I have returned to them their wealth and their slaves. And I return to you, O King, your ring, your throne, and your crown, on the condition that you do not rule except by my way and do not judge except by my judgment.' The King said to him: 'It is my pride and my honor that I do not rule except by your way and do not judge except by your judgment. If not for you, I would not have had the strength for this, nor would I have been guided to it. You have made my authority mighty and unassailable. I bear witness that there is no god but God alone, with no partner, and that you are His messenger. So, continue in the position I have entrusted to you, for you are with us established and trustworthy.'"

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 92

References: Al-Burhan

entries.arabic: عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ غِیَاثٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ (علیه السلام) قَالَ: کَانَ سِنِینُ یُوسُفَ الْغَلَاءَ الَّذِی أَصَابَ النَّاسَ وَ لَمْ یُتَمَنَ الْغَلَاءَ لِأَحَدٍ قَطُّ قَالَ فَأَتَاهُ التُّجَّارُ فَقَالُوا بِعْنَا قَالَ اشْتَرُوا فَقَالُوا نَأْخُذُ کَذَا بِکَذَا فَقَالَ خُذُوا وَ أَمَرَ فَکَالُوهُمْ فَحَمَلُوا وَ مَضَوْا حَتَّی دَخَلُوا الْمَدِینَهًَْ فَلَقِیَهُمْ قَوْمٌ تُجَّارٌ فَقَالُوا لَهُمْ کَیْفَ أَخَذْتُمْ فَقَالُوا کَذَا بِکَذَا وَ أَضْعَفُوا الثَّمَنَ قَالَ فَقَدِمُوا أُولَئِکَ عَلَی یُوسُفَ فَقَالُوا بِعْنَا فَقَالَ اشْتَرُوا کَیْفَ تَأْخُذُونَ فَقَالُوا بِعْنَا کَمَا بِعْتَ کَذَا بِکَذَا فَقَالَ مَا هُوَ کَمَا تَقُولُونَ وَ لَکِنْ خُذُوا فَأَخُذُوا ثُمَّ مَضَوْا حَتَّی دَخَلُوا الْمَدِینَهًَْ فَلَقِیَهُمْ آخَرُونَ فَقَالُوا کَیْفَ أَخَذْتُمْ فَقَالُوا کَذَا بِکَذَا وَ أَضْعَفُوا الثَّمَنَ قَالَ فَعَظُمَ النَّاسَ ذَلِکَ الْغَلَاءُ وَ قَالُوا اذْهَبُوا بِنَا حَتَّی نَشْتَرِیَ قَالَ فَذَهَبُوا إِلَی یُوسُفَ فَقَالُوا بِعْنَا فَقَالَ اشْتَرُوا فَقَالُوا بِعْنَا کَمَا بِعْتَ فَقَالَ وَ کَیْفَ بِعْتُ قَالُوا کَذَا بِکَذَا فَقَالَ مَا هُوَ کَذَلِکَ وَ لَکِنْ خُذُوا فَأَخُذُوا وَ رَجَعُوا إِلَی الْمَدِینَهًِْ فَأَخْبَرُوا النَّاسَ فَقَالُوا فِیمَا بَیْنَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا حَتَّی نَکْذِبَ فِی الرَّخْصِ کَمَا کَذَبْنَا فِی الْغَلَاءِ قَالَ فَذَهَبُوا إِلَی یُوسُفَ فَقَالُوا لَهُ بِعْنَا فَقَالَ اشْتَرُوا فَقَالُوا بِعْنَا کَمَا بِعْتَ قَالَ وَ کَیْفَ بِعْتُ قَالُوا کَذَا بِکَذَا بِالْحَطِّ مِنَ السِّعْرِ الْأَوَّلِ فَقَالَ مَا هُوَ کَذَا وَ لَکِنْ خُذُوا قَالَ فَأَخُذُوا وَ ذَهَبُوا إِلَی الْمَدِینَهًِْ فَلَقِیَهُمْ النَّاسُ فَسَأَلُوهُمْ بِکَمِ اشْتَرَیْتُمْ فَقَالُوا کَذَا بِکَذَا بِنِصْفِ الْحَطِّ الْأَوَّلِ فَقَالَ الْآخَرُونَ اذْهَبُوا بِنَا حَتَّی نَشْتَرِیَ فَذَهَبُوا إِلَی یُوسُفَ فَقَالُوا بِعْنَا فَقَالَ اشْتَرُوا فَقَالُوا بِعْنَا کَمَا بِعْتَ فَقَالَ وَ کَیْفَ بِعْتُ فَقَالُوا کَذَا بِکَذَا بِالْحَطِّ مِنَ النِّصْفِ فَقَالَ مَا هُوَ کَمَا تَقُولُونَ وَ لَکِنْ خُذُوا فَلَمْ یَزَالُوا یَتَکَاذَبُونَ حَتَّی رَجَعَ السِّعْرُ إِلَی الْأَمْرِ الْأَوَّلِ کَمَا أَرَادَ اللَّه.
entries.persian: حفص‌بن‌غیاث نقل می‌کند که امام صادق (علیه السلام) فرمود: «یوسف (علیه السلام) بر گرانی‌ای که مردم به آن دچار شدند، پیشی گرفت و گرانی را هرگز برای هیچ‌کس نخواست». حضرت فرمود: «بازرگانان نزد او آمدند و گفتند: «به ما [آذوقه] بفروش». یوسف (علیه السلام) فرمود: «بخرید»! آنان گفتند «ما فلان مقدار را به فلان قیمت می‌خریم». یوسف (علیه السلام) فرمود: «بخرید و دستور داد تا برایشان پیمانه کنند. آنان آن مقدار را برداشتند و رفتند تا اینکه وارد شهر شدند». گروهی از بازرگانان به آنان برخوردند و پرسیدند: «چگونه خریدید»؟ آنان پاسخ دادند: «فلان مقدار را به فلان قیمت خریدیم». و قیمت را چند برابر کردند. حضرت فرمود: «آنان بر یوسف (علیه السلام) وارد شدند و گفتند: «به ما بفروش». یوسف (علیه السلام) فرمود: «بخرید، چگونه می‌خرید». گفتند: «بفروش! همچنان‌که فلان مقدار را به فلان قیمت فروختی»! یوسف فرمود: «آن‌طور که می‌گویند نیست. امّا بخرید». آنان خریدند سپس رفتند تا اینکه وارد شهر شدند، گروه دیگری به آنان برخوردند و پرسیدند: «چگونه خریدید»؟ آنان پاسخ دادند: «فلان مقدار را به فلان قیمت»! و قیمت را چند برابر کردند. حضرت فرمود: «مردم آن قیمت را زیاد دانستند». و گفتند: «بیایید برویم و بخریم»! حضرت فرمود: «آنان به‌سوی یوسف (علیه السلام) رفتند و گفتند: به ما [نیز] بفروش»! یوسف (علیه السلام) فرمود: «بخرید»! آنان گفتند: «به ما بفروش همان‌طور که فروختی». یوسف (علیه السلام) فرمود: «آن‌طور که می‌گویید نیست، امّا بخرید». حضرت فرمود: «آنان خریدند و به شهر بازگشتند. آنان مردم را باخبر کردند و با خودشان گفتند که بیایید تا قیمت را به دروغ ارزان کنیم، همان‌طور که به دروغ گران کردیم». حضرت فرمود: آنان نزد یوسف (علیه السلام) آمدند و گفتند: «به ما بفروش»! یوسف (علیه السلام) فرمود: «بخرید». آنان گفتند همان‌طور که فروختی بفروش». یوسف (علیه السلام) پرسید: «من چگونه فروختم»! آنان پاسخ دادند: «فلان مقدار به فلان قیمت». و از قیمت اوّل کاستند. یوسف (علیه السلام) فرمود: «این‌طور که می‌گویید نیست، امّا بخرید». حضرت فرمود: «آنان خریدند و به شهر رفتند». مردم به آنان برخوردند و از آنان پرسیدند: «به چه قیمتی خریدید»؟ آنان پاسخ دادند: «فلان مقدار به فلان قیمت». به نصف قیمت اوّل. دیگران گفتند: «بیایید برویم تا بخریم». آنان نزد یوسف (علیه السلام) آمدند و گفتند: «بفروش» یوسف (علیه السلام) فرمود: «بخرید». آنان گفتند: «همان‌طور که فروختی بفروش». یوسف (علیه السلام) پرسید: «من چگونه فروختم»؟ آنان پاسخ دادند: «فلان مقدار به فلان قیمت». و از آن نصف نیز کاستند. یوسف (علیه السلام) فرمود: «آن‌طور که می‌گویید نیست، امّا بخرید». آنان پیوسته به یکدیگر دروغ می‌گفتند تا آنکه قیمت همان‌طور که خداوند خواست به همان حالت اوّل بازگشت».
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۹۴
entries.references: مستدرک الوسایل، ج۱۳، ص۲۷۸/ البرهان/ نورالثقلین
entries.english_translation:
Attribution:
Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope:
(Yusuf/ 57)

English translation:
Hafs ibn Ghiyath narrates that Abu Abd Allah (peace be upon him) said: "Yusuf (peace be upon him) preceded the famine that afflicted the people, and he never desired famine for anyone at all." He said: "The merchants came to him and said, 'Sell to us.' He said, 'Buy.' They said, 'We will take such-and-such for such-and-such price.' He said, 'Take it,' and he ordered that it be measured for them. They loaded it and departed until they entered the city. A group of merchants met them and asked, 'How did you buy it?' They replied, 'For such-and-such price,' and they inflated the price. He said: "They came to Yusuf and said, 'Sell to us.' He said, 'Buy. How will you buy?' They said, 'Sell to us as you sold [before], such-and-such for such-and-such price.' He said, 'It is not as you say, but take it.' So they took it, then departed until they entered the city. Another group met them and asked, 'How did you buy it?' They replied, 'For such-and-such price,' and they inflated the price. He said: "The people found that price to be exorbitant, and they said, 'Let us go and buy.' He said: "They went to Yusuf and said, 'Sell to us.' He said, 'Buy.' They said, 'Sell to us as you sold [before].' He said, 'And how did I sell?' They said, 'Such-and-such for such-and-such price.' He said, 'It is not like that, but take it.' He said: "They bought it and returned to the city. They informed the people and said among themselves, 'Come, let us lie about the price being cheap, just as we lied about it being expensive.' He said: "They went to Yusuf and said to him, 'Sell to us.' He said, 'Buy.' They said, 'Sell to us as you sold [before].' He said, 'And how did I sell?' They said, 'Such-and-such for such-and-such price,' reducing the initial price. He said, 'It is not like that, but take it.' He said: "They took it and went to the city. The people met them and asked, 'At what price did you buy?' They replied, 'For such-and-such price,' at half the initial reduction. The others said, 'Let us go and buy.' They went to Yusuf and said, 'Sell to us.' He said, 'Buy.' They said, 'Sell to us as you sold [before].' He said, 'And how did I sell?' They said, 'Such-and-such for such-and-such price,' reducing it from the half. He said, 'It is not as you say, but take it.' They continued to lie to one another until the price returned to the original state, just as God willed."

Book:
Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 94

References:
Mustadrak al-Wasa'il, Vol. 13, p. 278; Al-Burhan; Nur al-Thaqalayn

entries.arabic: لَمَّا صَارَتِ الْأَشْیَاءُ لِیُوسُفَ بْنِ یَعْقُوبَ (علیه السلام) جَعَلَ الطَّعَامَ فِی بُیُوتٍ وَ أَمَرَ بَعْضَ وُکَلَائِهِ فَکَانَ یَقُولُ بِعْ بِکَذَا وَ کَذَا وَ السِّعْرُ قَائِمٌ فَلَمَّا عَلِمَ أَنَّهُ یَزِیدُ فِی ذَلِکَ الْیَوْمِ کَرِهَ أَنْ یَجْرِیَ الْغَلَاءُ عَلَی لِسَانِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ اذْهَبْ فَبِعْ وَ لَمْ یُسَمِّ لَهُ سِعْراً فَذَهَبَ الْوَکِیلُ غَیْرَ بَعِیدٍ ثُمَّ رَجَعَ إِلَیْهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ اذْهَبْ فَبِعْ وَ کَرِهَ أَنْ یَجْرِیَ الْغَلَاءُ عَلَی لِسَانِهِ فَذَهَبَ الْوَکِیلُ فَجَاءَ أَوَّلُ مَنِ اکْتَالَ فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ دُونَ مَا کَانَ بِالْأَمْسِ بِمِکْیَالٍ قَالَ الْمُشْتَرِی حَسْبُکَ إِنَّمَا أَرَدْتُ بِکَذَا وَ کَذَا فَعَلِمَ الْوَکِیلُ أَنَّهُ قَدْ غَلَا بِمِکْیَالٍ ثُمَّ جَاءَهُ آخَرُ فَقَالَ لَهُ کِلْ لِی فَکَالَ فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ دُونَ الَّذِی کَالَ لِلْأَوَّلِ بِمِکْیَالٍ قَالَ لَهُ الْمُشْتَرِی حَسْبُکَ إِنَّمَا أَرَدْتُ بِکَذَا وَ کَذَا فَعَلِمَ الْوَکِیلُ أَنَّهُ قَدْ غَلَا بِمِکْیَالٍ حَتَّی صَارَ إِلَی وَاحِدٍ بِوَاحِدٍ.
entries.persian: وقتی کارها به دست یوسف (علیه السلام) افتاد، مواد خوراکی را در خانه‌هایی قرار داده و به یکی از کارگزاران خود دستور داد آن‌ها را به قیمت ثابت و مشخّصی بفروشد. زمانی‌که یوسف (علیه السلام) متوجّه شد قیمت‌ها بالاتر می‌رود، چون نمی‌خواست سخن او باعث گرانی شود، از این‌رو [روز بعد] بدون آنکه سخنی از قیمت به میان بیاورد به کارگزار خود گفت: «برو بفروش». کارگزار اندکی دور شد و نزد یوسف (علیه السلام) بازگشت. [برای بار دوّم] یوسف (علیه السلام) به او گفت: برو بفروش». و نمی‌خواست سخنش باعث گرانی شود. سرانجام کارگزار رفت. اوّلین خریدار که آمد، هنگام اندازه‌گیریِ کالا وقتی مقدار آن به یک پیمانه کمتر از مقدار روز گذشته رسید. خریدار گفت: «کافی است! من همین مقدار در مقابل فلان مبلغ می‌خواستم». کارگزار فهمید که [آن کالا] به مقدار یک پیمانه گران‌تر شده است. کمی بعد، خریدار دیگری آمد و درخواست کالا کرد و کارگزار مشغول اندازه‌گیری شد؛ وقتی به یک پیمانه کمتر از مقداری که برای مشتری قبلی پیمانه کرده بود رسید. مشتری گفت: «کافی است! من همین مقدار در مقابل فلان مبلغ می‌خواستم. بازکارگزار فهمید که [آن کالا] به مقدار یک پیمانه گران‌تر شده است. [و این روند افزایش قیمت ادامه یافت] تا اینکه آن مبلغ با یک پیمانه از آن کالا برابر شد».
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۹۶
entries.references: الکافی، ج۵، ص۱۶۳/ نورالثقلین
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Yusuf/ 57)

English translation: When matters came under the control of Yusuf ibn Ya'qub (peace be upon him), he placed the food in storehouses and instructed one of his agents, saying, "Sell it for such and such," while the price was established. When he realized that the price was increasing that day, he disliked that inflation should be spoken of by his tongue. So he said to him, "Go and sell," without specifying a price for him. The agent went a short distance, then returned to him. He said to him, "Go and sell," for he disliked that inflation should be spoken of by his tongue. The agent went away. The first person to come for measuring arrived, and when it reached one measure less than what it had been the previous day, the buyer said, "That is enough for you; I only wanted such and such [amount] for such and such [price]." The agent realized that it had become more expensive by one measure. Then another came to him and said, "Measure for me." He measured, and when it reached one measure less than what he had measured for the first customer, the buyer said to him, "That is enough for you; I only wanted such and such [amount] for such and such [price]." The agent realized that it had become more expensive by one measure, until it reached the point where one [measure] was [sold] for one [price].

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 96

References: Al-Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 163; Nur al-Thaqalayn

entries.arabic: مَلَکَ یُوسُفُ مِصْرَ وَ بَرَارِیَهَا لَمْ یُجَاوِزْهَا إِلَی غَیْرِهَا.
entries.persian: قلمروی حکومت یوسف (علیه السلام)، کشور مصر و صحراهای آن بود و از آن محدوده، خارج نبود.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۹۶
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۱۲، ص۳۰۵/ نورالثقلین/ البرهان
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Yusuf/ 57)

English translation: Yusuf ruled over Egypt and its deserts; he did not extend beyond them to any other territory.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 96

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 12, p. 305; Nur al-Thaqalayn; al-Burhan

verses.arabic: وَجَآءَ إِخْوَةُ يُوسُفَ فَدَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَعَرَفَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُۥ مُنكِرُونَ
verses.english: [After some years] the brothers of Joseph came and entered his presence. He recognized them, but they did not recognize him.
verses.transliteration_html: Wajaa ikhwatu yoosufa fadakhaloo AAalayhi faAAarafahum wahum lahu munkiroona
verses.transliteration_text: Wajaa ikhwatu yoosufa fadakhaloo AAalayhi faAAarafahum wahum lahu munkiroona

commentary.plain_text: No commentary available for this verse

verses.arabic: وَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُم بِجَهَازِهِمْ قَالَ ٱئْتُونِى بِأَخٍۢ لَّكُم مِّنْ أَبِيكُمْ ۚ أَلَا تَرَوْنَ أَنِّىٓ أُوفِى ٱلْكَيْلَ وَأَنَا۠ خَيْرُ ٱلْمُنزِلِينَ
verses.english: When he had furnished them with their provision, he said, ‘Bring me a brother that you have through your father. Do you not see that I give the full measure and that I am the best of hosts?
verses.transliteration_html: Walamma jahhazahum bijahazihim qala itoonee biakhin lakum min abeekum ala tarawna annee oofee alkayla waana khayru almunzileena
verses.transliteration_text: Walamma jahhazahum bijahazihim qala itoonee biakhin lakum min abeekum ala tarawna annee oofee alkayla waana khayru almunzileena

commentary.plain_text: No commentary available for this verse

verses.arabic: فَإِن لَّمْ تَأْتُونِى بِهِۦ فَلَا كَيْلَ لَكُمْ عِندِى وَلَا تَقْرَبُونِ
verses.english: But if you do not bring him to me, then there will be no rations for you with me, and don’t [ever] come near me.’
verses.transliteration_html: Fain lam tatoonee bihi fala kayla lakum AAindee wala taqrabooni
verses.transliteration_text: Fain lam tatoonee bihi fala kayla lakum AAindee wala taqrabooni

commentary.plain_text: No commentary available for this verse

verses.arabic: قَالُوا۟ سَنُرَٰوِدُ عَنْهُ أَبَاهُ وَإِنَّا لَفَـٰعِلُونَ
verses.english: They said, ‘We will solicit him from his father. [That] we will surely do.’
verses.transliteration_html: Qaloo sanurawidu AAanhu abahu wainna lafaAAiloona
verses.transliteration_text: Qaloo sanurawidu AAanhu abahu wainna lafaAAiloona

commentary.plain_text: No commentary available for this verse

verses.arabic: وَقَالَ لِفِتْيَـٰنِهِ ٱجْعَلُوا۟ بِضَـٰعَتَهُمْ فِى رِحَالِهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَعْرِفُونَهَآ إِذَا ٱنقَلَبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
verses.english: He said to his servants, ‘Put their money [back] into their saddlebags. Maybe they will recognize it when they return to their folks, and maybe they will come back [again].’
verses.transliteration_html: Waqala lifityanihi ijAAaloo bidaAAatahum fee rihalihim laAAallahum yaAArifoonaha itha inqalaboo ila ahlihim laAAallahum yarjiAAoona
verses.transliteration_text: Waqala lifityanihi ijAAaloo bidaAAatahum fee rihalihim laAAallahum yaAArifoonaha itha inqalaboo ila ahlihim laAAallahum yarjiAAoona

entries.arabic: وَ کَانَ بَیْنَهُ وَ بَیْنَ أَبِیهِ ثَمَانِیَهًَْ عَشَرَ یَوْماً وَ کَانَ فِی بَادِیَهًٍْ وَ کَانَ النَّاسُ مِنَ الْآفَاقِ یَخْرُجُونَ إِلَی مِصْرَ لِیَمْتَارُوا طَعَاماً وَ کَانَ یَعْقُوبُ (علیه السلام) وَ وُلْدُهُ نُزُولًا فِی بَادِیَهًٍْ فِیهِ مُقْلٌ فَأَخَذَ إِخْوَهًُْ یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) مِنْ ذَلِکَ الْمُقْلِ وَ حَمَلُوهُ إِلَی مِصْرَ لِیَمْتَارُوا بِهِ طَعَاماً وَ کَانَ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) یَتَوَلَّی الْبَیْعَ بِنَفْسِهِ فَلَمَّا دَخَلَ إِخْوَتُهُ عَلَی یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) عَرَفَهُمْ وَ لَمْ یَعْرِفُوهُ کَمَا حَکَی اللَّهُ عَزَّوَجَلَّ وَ هُمْ لَهُ مُنْکِرُونَ* فَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُمْ بِجَهَازِهِمْ وَ أَعْطَاهُمْ وَ أَحْسَنَ إِلَیْهِمْ فِی الْکَیْلِ قَالَ لَهُمْ مَنْ أَنْتُمْ قَالُوا نَحْنُ بَنُو یَعْقُوبَ‌بْنِ‌إِسْحَاقَ‌بْنِ‌إِبْرَاهِیمَ (علیه السلام) خَلِیلِ اللَّهِ الَّذِی أَلْقَاهُ نُمْرُودُ فِی النَّارِ فَلَمْ یَحْتَرِقْ فَجَعَلَهَا اللَّهُ عَلَیْهِ بَرْداً وَ سَلَاماً قَالَ فَمَا فَعَلَ أَبُوکُمْ قَالُوا شَیْخٌ ضَعِیفٌ قَالَ فَلَکُمْ أَخٌ غَیْرُکُمْ قَالُوا لَنَا أَخٌ مِنْ أَبِینَا لَا مِنْ أُمِّنَا قَالَ فَإِذَا رَجَعْتُمْ إِلَیَّ فَأْتُوْنِی بِهِ وَ هُوَ قَوْلُهُ ائْتُونِی بِأَخٍ لَکُمْ مِنْ أَبِیکُمْ أَ لا تَرَوْنَ أَنِّی أُوفِی الْکَیْلَ وَ أَنَا خَیْرُ الْمُنْزِلِینَ* فَإِنْ لَمْ تَأْتُونِی بِهِ فَلا کَیْلَ لَکُمْ عِنْدِی وَ لا تَقْرَبُونِ* قالُوا سَنُراوِدُ عَنْهُ أَباهُ وَ إِنَّا لَفاعِلُونَ ثُمَّ قَالَ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) لِقَوْمِهِ رُدُّوا هَذِهِ الْبِضَاعَهًَْ الَّتِی حَمَلُوهَا إِلَیْنَا اجْعَلُوهَا فِیمَا بَیْنَ رِحَالِهِمْ حَتَّی إِذَا رَجَعُوا إِلَی مَنَازِلِهِمْ وَ رَأَوْهَا رَجَعُوا إِلَیْنَا وَ هُوَ قَوْلُهُ وَ قالَ لِفِتْیانِهِ اجْعَلُوا بِضاعَتَهُمْ فِی رِحالِهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ یَعْرِفُونَها إِذَا انْقَلَبُوا إِلی أَهْلِهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ یَرْجِعُونَ یَعْنِی کَیْ یَرْجِعُونَ{یَرْجِعُوا} فَلَمَّا رَجَعُوا إِلی أَبِیهِمْ قالُوا یا أَبانا مُنِعَ مِنَّا الْکَیْلُ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعَنا أَخانا نَکْتَلْ وَ إِنَّا لَهُ لَحافِظُونَ* قالَ یَعْقُوبُ هَلْ آمَنُکُمْ عَلَیْهِ إِلَّا کَما أَمِنْتُکُمْ عَلی أَخِیهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ فَاللهُ خَیْرٌحافِظاً وَ هُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِینَ* وَ لَمَّا فَتَحُوا مَتاعَهُمْ وَجَدُوا بِضاعَتَهُمْ رُدَّتْ إِلَیْهِمْ فِی رِحَالِهِمُ الَّتِی حَمَلُوهَا إِلَی مِصْرَ قالُوا یا أَبانا ما نَبْغِی أَیْ مَا نُرِیدُ هذِهِ بِضاعَتُنا رُدَّتْ إِلَیْنا وَ نَمِیرُ أَهْلَنا وَ نَحْفَظُ أَخانا وَ نَزْدادُ کَیْلَ بَعِیرٍ ذلِکَ کَیْلٌ یَسِیرٌ قالَ یَعْقُوبُ (علیه السلام) لَنْ أُرْسِلَهُ مَعَکُمْ حَتَّی تُؤْتُونِ مَوْثِقاً مِنَ اللهِ لَتَأْتُنَّنِی بِهِ إِلَّا أَنْ یُحاطَ بِکُمْ فَلَمَّا آتَوْهُ مَوْثِقَهُمْ قالَ یَعْقُوبُ اللهُ عَلی ما نَقُولُ وَکِیلٌ* فَخَرَجُوا وَ قَالَ لَهُمْ یَعْقُوبُ لاتَدْخُلُوا مِنْ بابٍ واحِدٍ وَ ادْخُلُوا مِنْ أَبْوابٍ مُتَفَرِّقَةٍ وَ ما أُغْنِی عَنْکُمْ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ شَیْءٍ إِنِ الْحُکْمُ إِلَّا للهِ عَلَیْهِ تَوَکَّلْتُ وَ عَلَیْهِ فَلْیَتَوَکَّلِ الْمُتَوَکِّلُونَ* وَ لَمَّا دَخَلُوا مِنْ حَیْثُ أَمَرَهُمْ أَبُوهُمْ ما کانَ یُغْنِی عَنْهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ شَیْءٍ إِلَّا حاجَةً فِی نَفْسِ یَعْقُوب قَضاها وَ إِنَّهُ لَذُو عِلْمٍ لِما عَلَّمْناهُ وَ لکِنَّ أَکْثَرَ النَّاسِ لایَعْلَمُونَ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۹۶
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۱۲، ص۲۳۶/ القمی، ج۱، ص۳۴۶
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Ali ibn Ibrahim (may Allah have mercy upon him)

Scope: (Yusuf/62)

English translation: There was a distance of eighteen days between Yusuf (peace be upon him) and his father, and he (Yaqub) was living in the desert. People from all horizons were traveling to Egypt to obtain food. Yaqub (peace be upon him) and his sons were staying in a desert area where there was a medicinal plant called "muql." The brothers of Yusuf (peace be upon him) took some of this muql and carried it to Egypt to trade it for food. Yusuf (peace be upon him) was personally overseeing the sales. When his brothers entered upon Yusuf (peace be upon him), he recognized them, but they did not recognize him, just as Allah the Almighty has narrated: "And they were unaware of him."

When he had provided them with their provisions and given to them, and had been generous to them in the measure, he said to them, "Who are you?" They replied, "We are the sons of Yaqub ibn Ishaq ibn Ibrahim (peace be upon him), the Friend of Allah, whom Nimrod cast into the fire, but he did not burn, for Allah made it cool and safe for him." He asked, "What has become of your father?" They said, "He is an old, weak man." He asked, "Do you have a brother other than yourselves?" They said, "We have a brother from our father, not from our mother." He said, "When you return to me, bring him to me." This is the meaning of His words: "Bring me a brother of yours from your father. Do you not see that I give full measure and that I am the best of hosts? But if you do not bring him to me, there shall be no measure for you with me, nor shall you come near me." They said, "We will attempt to persuade his father about him, and we will certainly do it."

Then Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to his people, "Return this merchandise which they brought to us; place it within their saddlebags so that when they return to their homes and see it, they will return to us." This is the meaning of His words: "And he said to his servants, 'Put their merchandise into their saddlebags so that they may recognize it when they return to their families, perhaps they will return.'" That is, so that they might return.

When they returned to their father, they said, "O our father, the measure is withheld from us, so send our brother with us that we may get our measure, and we will indeed be his guardians." Yaqub said, "Shall I entrust him to you except as I entrusted you with his brother before? But Allah is the best guardian, and He is the Most Merciful of the merciful." And when they opened their baggage, they found their merchandise had been returned to them in their saddlebags which they had carried to Egypt. They said, "O our father, what more could we desire?"—meaning, what more do we want? "This is our merchandise returned to us, and we will provide for our family, and we will guard our brother, and we will obtain an extra camel-load; that is a small measure."

Yaqub (peace be upon him) said, "I will never send him with you until you give me a solemn oath by Allah that you will surely bring him back to me, unless you are surrounded." And when they had given him their solemn oath, Yaqub said, "Allah is witness over what we say."

When they were about to depart, Yaqub said to them, "Do not enter from one gate, but enter from different gates; and I cannot avail you against Allah at all. The decision is only for Allah; upon Him I have relied, and upon Him let the reliant rely." And when they entered from where their father had ordered them, it did not avail them against Allah at all, except for a need in the soul of Yaqub which he fulfilled. And indeed, he was possessed of knowledge because of what We had taught him, but most of the people do not know.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 96

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 12, p. 236; Al-Qummi, Vol. 1, p. 346

entries.arabic: لَمَّا أَصَابَ آلَ یَعْقُوبَ (علیه السلام) مَا أَصَابَ النَّاسَ مِنْ ضِیقِ الطَّعَامِ جَمَعَ یَعْقُوبُ (علیه السلام) بَنِیهِ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ یَا بَنِیَّ إِنَّهُ بَلَغَنِی أَنَّهُ یُبَاعُ بِمِصْرَ طَعَامٌ طَیِّبٌ وَ أَنَّ صَاحِبَهُ رَجُلٌ صَالِحٌ لَا یَحْبِسُ النَّاسَ فَاذْهَبُوا إِلَیْهِ وَ اشْتَرُوْا مِنْهُ طَعَاماً فَإِنَّهُ سَیُحْسِنُ إِلَیْکُمْ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ فَتَجَهَّزُوا وَ سَارُوا حَتَّی وَرَدُوا مِصْرَ فَأُدْخِلُوا عَلَی یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) فَعَرَفَهُمْ وَ هُمْ لَهُ مُنْکِرُونَ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ مَنْ أَنْتُمْ قَالُوا نَحْنُ أَوْلَادُ یَعْقُوبَ‌بْنِ‌إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِیمَ (علیه السلام) خَلِیلِ الرَّحْمَنِ وَ نَحْنُ مِنْ جَبَلِ کَنْعَانَ قَالَ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) وَلَّدَکُمْ إِذاً ثَلَاثَهًُْ أَنْبِیَاءَ (وَ مَا أَنْتُمْ بِحُلَمَاءَ وَ لَا فِیکُمْ وَقَارٌ وَ لَا خُشُوعٌ فَلَعَلَّکُمْ جَوَاسِیسُ لِبَعْضِ الْمُلُوکِ جِئْتُمْ إِلَی بِلَادِی فَقَالُوا أَیُّهَا الْمَلِکُ لَسْنَا بِجَوَاسِیسَ وَ لَا أَصْحَابَ الْحَرْبِ وَ لَوْ تَعْلَمُ بِأَبِینَا إِذاً لَکَرُمْنَا عَلَیْکَ فَإِنَّهُ نَبِیُّ اللَّهِ وَ ابْنُ أَنْبِیَائِهِ (وَ إِنَّهُ لَمَحْزُونٌ قَالَ لَهُمْ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) فَمِمَّا حُزْنُهُ وَ هُوَ نَبِیُّ اللَّهِ وَ ابْنُ أَنْبِیَائِهِ وَ الْجَنَّهًُْ مَأْوَاهُ وَ هُوَ یَنْظُرُ إِلَیْکُمْ فِی مِثْلِ عَدَدِکُمْ وَ قُوَّتِکُمْ فَلَعَلَّ حُزْنَهُ إِنَّمَا هُوَ مِنْ قِبَلِ سَفَهِکُمْ وَ جَهْلِکُمْ وَ کَذِبِکُمْ وَ کَیْدِکُمْ وَ مَکْرِکُمْ قَالُوا أَیُّهَا الْمَلِکُ لَسْنَا بِجُهَّالٍ وَ لَا سُفَهَاءَ وَ لَا أَتَاهُ الْحُزْنُ مِنْ قِبَلِنَا وَ لَکِنْ کَانَ لَهُ ابْنٌ کَانَ أَصْغَرَنَا سِنّاً یُقَالُ لَهُ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) فَخَرَجَ مَعَنَا إِلَی الصَّیْدِ فَأَکَلَهُ الذِّئْبُ فَلَمْ یَزَلْ بَعْدَهُ کَئِیباً حَزِیناً بَاکِیاً فَقَالَ لَهُمْ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) کُلُّکُمْ مِنْ أَبٍ وَاحِدٍ قَالُوا أَبُونَا وَاحِدٌ وَ أُمَّهَاتُنَا شَتَّی قَالَ فَمَا حَمَلَ أَبَاکُمْ عَلَی أَنْ سَرَّحَکُمْ کُلَّکُمْ أَلَّا حَبَسَ مِنْکُمْ وَاحِداً یَأْنَسُ بِهِ وَ یَسْتَرِیحُ إِلَیْهِ قَالُوا قَدْ فَعَلَ قَدْ حَبَسَ مِنَّا وَاحِداً هُوَ أَصْغَرُنَا سِنّاً قَالَ وَ لِمَ اخْتَارَهُ لِنَفْسِهِ مِنْ بَیْنِکُمْ قَالُوا لِأَنَّهُ أَحَبُّ أَوْلَادِهِ إِلَیْهِ بَعْدَ یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) فَقَالَ لَهُمْ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) إِنِّی أَحْبِسُ مِنْکُمْ وَاحِداً یَکُونُ عِنْدِی وَ ارْجِعُوا إِلَی أَبِیکُمْ وَ أَقْرِءُوهُ مِنِّی السَّلَامَ وَ قُولُوا لَهُ یُرْسِلْ إِلَیَّ بِابْنِهِ الَّذِی زَعَمْتُمْ أَنَّهُ حَبَسَهُ عِنْدَهُ لِیُخْبِرَنِی عَنْ حُزْنِهِ مَا الَّذِی أَحْزَنَهُ وَ عَنْ سُرْعَهًِْ الشَّیْبِ إِلَیْهِ قَبْلَ أَوَانِ مَشِیبِهِ وَ عَنْ بُکَائِهِ وَ ذَهَابِ بَصَرِهِ فَلَمَّا قَالَ هَذَا اقْتَرَعُوا بَیْنَهُمْ فَخَرَجَتِ الْقُرْعَهًُْ عَلَی شَمْعُونَ فَأَمَرَ بِهِ فَحُبِسَ فَلَمَّا وَدَّعُوا شَمْعُونَ قَالَ لَهُمْ یَا إِخْوَتَاهْ انْظُرُوا مَا ذَا وَقَعْتُ فِیهِ وَ أَقْرِءُوا وَالِدِی مِنِّی السَّلَامَ فَوَدَّعُوهُ وَ سَارُوا حَتَّی وَرَدُوا الشَّامَ وَ دَخَلُوا عَلَی یَعْقُوبَ (علیه السلام) وَ سَلَّمُوا عَلَیْهِ سَلَاماً ضَعِیفاً فَقَالَ لَهُمْ یَا بَنِیَّ مَا لَکُمْ تُسَلِّمُونَ سَلَاماً ضَعِیفاً وَ مَا لِی لَا أَسْمَعُ فِیکُمْ صَوْتَ خَلِیلِی شَمْعُونَ قَالُوا یَا أَبَانَا إِنَّا جِئْنَاکَ مِنْ عِنْدِ أَعْظَمِ النَّاسِ مُلْکاً لَمْ یَرَ النَّاسُ مِثْلَهُ حُکْماً وَ عِلْماً وَ خُشُوعاً وَ سَکِینَهًًْ وَ وَقَاراً وَ لَئِنْ کَانَ لَکَ شَبِیهٌ فَإِنَّهُ لَشَبِیهُکَ وَ لَکِنَّا أَهْلُ بَیْتٍ خُلِقْنَا لِلْبَلَاءِ اتَّهَمَنَا الْمَلِکُ وَ زَعَمَ أَنَّهُ لَا یُصَدِّقُنَا حَتَّی تُرْسِلَ مَعَنَا بِابْنِ یَامِینَ بِرِسَالَهًٍْ مِنْکَ یُخْبِرُهُ عَنْ حُزْنِکَ وَ عَنْ سُرْعَهًِْ الشَّیْبِ إِلَیْکَ قَبْلَ أَوَانِ الْمَشِیبِ وَ عَنْ بُکَائِکَ وَ ذَهَابِ بَصَرِکَ فَظَنَّ یَعْقُوبُ (علیه السلام) أَنَّ ذَلِکَ مَکْرٌ مِنْهُمْ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ یَا بَنِیَّ بِئْسَ الْعَادَهًُْ عَادَتُکُمْ کُلَّمَا خَرَجْتُمْ فِی وَجْهٍ نَقَصَ مِنْکُمْ وَاحِدٌ لَا أُرْسِلُهُ مَعَکُمْ {فَ} لَمَّا فَتَحُوا مَتاعَهُمْ وَجَدُوا بِضاعَتَهُمْ رُدَّتْ إِلَیْهِمْ مِنْ غَیْرِ عِلْمٍ مِنْهُمْ أَقْبَلُوا إِلَی أَبِیهِمْ فَرِحِینَ قَالُوا یَا أَبَانَا مَا رَأَی النَّاسُ مِثْلَ هَذَا الْمَلِکِ أَشَدَّ اتِّقَاءً لِلْإِثْمِ مِنْهُ رَدَّ عَلَیْنَا بِضَاعَتَنَا مَخَافَهًَْ الْإِثْمِ وَ هِیَ بِضاعَتُنا رُدَّتْ إِلَیْنا وَ نَمِیرُ أَهْلَنا وَ نَحْفَظُ أَخانا وَ نَزْدادُ کَیْلَ بَعِیرٍ ذلِکَ کَیْلٌ یَسِیرٌ قَالَ یَعْقُوبُ (علیه السلام) قَدْ عَلِمْتُمْ أَنَّ ابْنَ یَامِینَ أَحَبُّکُمْ إِلَیَّ بَعْدَ أَخِیکُمْ یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) وَ بِهِ أُنْسِی وَ إِلَیْهِ سُکُونِی مِنْ بَیْنِ جَمَاعَتِکُمْ فَ لَنْ أُرْسِلَهُ مَعَکُمْ حَتَّی تُؤْتُونِ مَوْثِقاً مِنَ اللهِ لَتَأْتُنَّنِی بِهِ إِلَّا أَنْ یُحاطَ بِکُمْ فَضَمِنَهُ یَهُودَا فَخَرَجُوا حَتَّی وَرَدُوا مِصْرَ فَدَخَلُوا عَلَی یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) فَقَالَ لَهُمْ هَلْ بَلَّغْتُمْ رِسَالَتِی قَالُوا نَعَمْ وَ قَدْجِئْنَاکَ بِجَوَابِهَا مَعَ هَذَا الْغُلَامِ فَسَلْهُ عَمَّا بَدَا لَکَ قَالَ لَهُ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) بِمَا أَرْسَلَکَ أَبُوکَ إِلَیَّ یَا غُلَامُ قَالَ أَرْسَلَنِی إِلَیْکَ یُقْرِئُکَ السَّلَامَ وَ یَقُولُ إِنَّکَ أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَیَّ تَسْأَلُنِی عَنْ حُزْنِی وَ عَنْ سُرْعَهًِْ الشَّیْبِ إِلَیَّ قَبْلَ أَوَانِ الْمَشِیبِ وَ عَنْ بُکَائِی وَ ذَهَابِ بَصَرِی فَإِنَّ أَشَدَّ النَّاسِ حُزْناً وَ خَوْفاً أَذْکَرُهُمْ لِلْمَعَادِ وَ إِنَّمَا أَسْرَعَ الشَّیْبُ إِلَیَّ قَبْلَ أَوَانِ الْمَشِیبِ لِذِکْرِ یَوْمِ الْقِیَامَهًِْ وَ أَبْکَانِی وَ بَیَّضَ عَیْنِیَ الْحُزْنُ عَلَی حَبِیبِی یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) وَ قَدْ بَلَغَنِی حُزْنُکَ بِحُزْنِی وَ اهْتِمَامُکَ بِأَمْرِی فَکَانَ اللَّهُ لَکَ جَازِیاً وَ مُثِیباً وَ إِنَّکَ لَنْ تَصِلَنِی بِشَیْءٍ أَنَا أَشَدُّ فَرَحاً بِهِ مِنْ أَنْ تُعَجِّلَ عَلَیَّ وَلَدِی ابْنَ یَامِینَ فَإِنَّهُ أَحَبُّ أَوْلَادِی إِلَیَّ بَعْدَ یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) فَأُونِسَ بِهِ وَحْشَتِی وَ أَصِلَ بِهِ وَحْدَتِی تُعَجِّلَ عَلَیَّ بِمَا أَسْتَعِینُ بِهِ عَلَی عِیَالِی فَلَمَّا قَالَ هَذَا خَنَقَتْ یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) الْعَبْرَهًُْ وَ لَمْ یَصْبِرْ حَتَّی قَامَ فَدَخَلَ الْبَیْتَ وَ بَکَی سَاعَهًًْ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ إِلَیْهِمْ وَ أَمَرَ لَهُمْ بِطَعَامٍ وَ قَالَ لِیَجْلِسْ کُلُّ بَنِی‌أُمٍّ عَلَی مَائِدَهًٍْ فَجَلَسُوا وَ بَقِیَ ابْنُ یَامِینَ قَائِماً فَقَالَ لَهُ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) مَا لَکَ لَمْ تَجْلِسْ فَقَالَ لَهُ لَیْسَ لِی فِیهِمْ ابْنُ أُمٍّ فَقَالَ لَهُ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) أَ‌فَمَا کَانَ لَکَ ابْنُ أُمٍّ فَقَالَ لَهُ ابْنُ یَامِینَ بَلَی فَقَالَ لَهُ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) فَمَا فَعَلَ قَالَ زَعَمَ هَؤُلَاءِ أَنَّ الذِّئْبَ أَکَلَهُ قَالَ فَمَا بَلَغَ مِنْ حُزْنِکَ عَلَیْهِ قَالَ وُلِدَ لِی اثْنَا عَشَرَ ابْناً کُلُّهُمُ أَشْتَقُّ لَهُ اسْماً مِنِ اسْمِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) أَرَاکَ قَدْ عَانَقْتَ النِّسَاءَ وَ شَمَمْتَ الْوَلَدَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ ابْنُ یَامِینَ إِنَّ لِی أَباً صَالِحاً وَ إِنَّهُ قَالَ لِی تَزَوَّجْ لَعَلَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّوَجَلَّ یُخْرِجُ مِنْکَ ذُرِّیَّهًًْ یُثْقِلُ الْأَرْضَ بِالتَّسْبِیحِ فَقَالَ لَهُ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) تَعَالَ فَاجْلِسْ عَلَی مَائِدَتِی فَقَالَ إِخْوَهًُْ یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) لَقَدْ فَضَّلَ اللَّهُ یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) وَ أَخَاهُ حَتَّی إِنَّ الْمَلِکَ قَدْ أَجْلَسَهُ مَعَهُ عَلَی مَائِدَتِهِ فَأَمَرَ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) أَنْ یُجْعَلَ صُوَاعُ الْمَلِکِ فِی رَحْلِ ابْنِ یَامِینَ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۱۰۰
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۱۲، ص۲۵۶
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Ibn ‘Abbas (may Allah have mercy on him)

Scope: (Yusuf/ 62)

English translation: When the family of Ya‘qub (peace be upon him) was afflicted by the same food shortage that had affected the rest of the people, Ya‘qub (peace be upon him) gathered his sons and said to them, "O my sons, it has reached me that good food is being sold in Egypt and that its master is a righteous man who does not imprison people. Go to him and buy food from him, for he will surely be kind to you, if Allah wills." They prepared their provisions and traveled until they arrived in Egypt and were brought before Yusuf (peace be upon him). He recognized them, while they were unaware of who he was. He asked them, "Who are you?" They replied, "We are the sons of Ya‘qub ibn Ishaq ibn Ibrahim (peace be upon him), the Khalil of the Most Merciful, and we are from the mountains of Canaan." Yusuf (peace be upon him) said, "Then three prophets have sired you, yet you lack forbearance, dignity, and humility. Perhaps you are spies for some king who have come to my lands?" They said, "O King, we are not spies, nor are we people of war. If you knew our father, you would honor us, for he is a Prophet of Allah and the son of His prophets, and he is a man of great sorrow." Yusuf (peace be upon him) asked them, "What is the cause of his sorrow, when he is a Prophet of Allah and the son of His prophets, and Paradise is his abode? He looks upon you, who are many in number and strength; perhaps his sorrow is due to your foolishness, ignorance, lies, deceit, and craftiness?" They said, "O King, we are not ignorant or foolish, and his sorrow did not come from us. Rather, he had a son who was the youngest of us, called Yusuf (peace be upon him). He went out with us to hunt, and the wolf ate him. Since then, he has remained depressed, sorrowful, and weeping." Yusuf (peace be upon him) asked them, "Are you all from one father?" They replied, "Our father is one, but our mothers are different." He asked, "What compelled your father to send all of you and not keep one of you back to be his companion and source of comfort?" They said, "He has done so; he kept one of us back, the youngest of us." He asked, "Why did he choose him for himself from among you?" They said, "Because he is the most beloved of his children to him after Yusuf (peace be upon him)." Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to them, "I will detain one of you to stay with me. Return to your father and convey my greetings to him, and tell him to send me his son whom you claim he kept with him, so that he may inform me about his sorrow—what has saddened him, the cause of his hair turning gray before its time, his weeping, and the loss of his sight." When he said this, they cast lots among themselves, and the lot fell upon Sham‘un. He ordered him to be detained. When they bade farewell to Sham‘un, he said to them, "O my brothers, look at what has befallen me, and convey my greetings to my father." They bade him farewell and traveled until they reached the Levant and entered upon Ya‘qub (peace be upon him), greeting him with weak voices. He said to them, "O my sons, why do you greet me with weak voices, and why do I not hear the voice of my beloved Sham‘un among you?" They said, "O our father, we have come to you from the greatest of kings in terms of dominion; people have never seen his like in judgment, knowledge, humility, tranquility, and dignity. If you had an equal, it would be him. But we are a household created for trials. The King accused us and claimed he would not believe us until you send Binyamin with us, along with a message from you informing him of your sorrow, the cause of your hair turning gray before its time, your weeping, and the loss of your sight." Ya‘qub (peace be upon him) suspected that this was a ruse of theirs and said to them, "O my sons, what a wretched habit you have! Every time you go out on a journey, one of you is lost. I will not send him with you." When they opened their luggage, they found their goods had been returned to them without their knowledge. They went to their father, rejoicing, and said, "O our father, people have never seen a king like this, more fearful of sin than he; he returned our goods to us out of fear of sin. Here is our merchandise returned to us, and we will provide for our family, protect our brother, and gain an extra camel-load; that is an easy load." Ya‘qub (peace be upon him) said, "You know that Binyamin is the most beloved of you to me after your brother Yusuf (peace be upon him), and he is my comfort and my solace among you all. I will never send him with you until you give me a solemn oath by Allah that you will surely bring him back to me, unless you are surrounded." Yahuda guaranteed it, and they went out until they arrived in Egypt and entered upon Yusuf (peace be upon him). He said to them, "Have you delivered my message?" They said, "Yes, and we have brought you the answer with this boy; ask him about whatever occurs to you." Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to the boy, "With what message did your father send you to me?" He said, "He sent me to you to convey his greetings and to say: 'You wrote to me asking about my sorrow, the cause of my hair turning gray before its time, my weeping, and the loss of my sight. Indeed, the most sorrowful and fearful of people are those who most remember the Hereafter. My hair turned gray before its time only because of the remembrance of the Day of Resurrection, and the sorrow for my beloved Yusuf (peace be upon him) has made me weep and whitened my eyes. It has reached me that you are saddened by my sorrow and concerned for my affairs; may Allah reward and recompense you. The best thing you can do to please me is to hasten my son Binyamin back to me, for he is the most beloved of my children to me after Yusuf (peace be upon him); I find comfort in him and solace in my loneliness. Hasten to me with that which will help me support my family.'" When he said this, Yusuf (peace be upon him) was choked with tears and could not restrain himself; he stood up, entered the house, and wept for a while. Then he went out to them and ordered food for them, saying, "Let every brother from the same mother sit at one table." They sat, but Binyamin remained standing. Yusuf (peace be upon him) asked him, "Why have you not sat?" He replied, "I have no brother from the same mother among them." Yusuf (peace be upon him) asked, "Did you not have a brother from your mother?" Binyamin replied, "Yes." Yusuf (peace be upon him) asked, "What happened to him?" He said, "These people claim that a wolf ate him." He asked, "How deep is your sorrow for him?" He said, "I have had twelve sons, and I have derived each of their names from his name." Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to him, "I see that you have embraced women and smelled children after him." Binyamin replied, "I have a righteous father who told me, 'Marry, perhaps Allah the Almighty will bring forth from you a progeny that will fill the earth with His praise.'" Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to him, "Come and sit at my table." The brothers of Yusuf (peace be upon him) said, "Allah has favored Yusuf (peace be upon him) and his brother, to the extent that the King has seated him with him at his table." Yusuf (peace be upon him) then ordered that the King's drinking cup be placed in the luggage of Binyamin.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 100

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 12, p. 256

entries.arabic: فَأَمَرَ یُوسُفُ أَنْ یَبْنِیَ کَنَادِیجَ مِنْ صَخْرٍ وَ طَیَّنَهَا بِالْکِلْسِ ثُمَّ أَمَرَ بِزُرُوعِ مِصْرَ فَحُصِدَتْ وَ دَفَعَ إِلَی کُلِّ إِنْسَانٍ حِصَّتَهُ وَ تَرَکَ الْبَاقِیَ فِی سُنْبُلِهِ لَمْ یَدُسْهُ، فَوَضَعَهَا فِی الْکَنَادِیجِ فَفَعَلَ ذَلِکَ سَبْعَ سِنِینَ فَلَمَّا جَاءَ سِنِی الْجَدْبِ فَکَانَ یَخْرُجُ السُّنْبُلُ فَیَبِیعُ بِمَا شَاءَ، وَ کَانَ بَیْنَهُ وَ بَیْنَ أَبِیهِ ثَمَانِیَهًَْ عَشَرَ یَوْماً وَ کَانُوا فِی بَادِیَهًٍْ وَ کَانَ النَّاسُ مِنَ الْآفَاقِ یَخْرُجُونَ إِلَی مِصْرَ لِیَمْتَارُوا طَعَاماً وَ کَانَ یَعْقُوبُ وَ وُلْدُهُ نُزُولًا فِی بَادِیَهًٍْ فِیهِ مُقْلٌ فَأَخَذَ إِخْوَهًُْ یُوسُفَ مِنْ ذَلِکَ الْمُقْلِ وَ حَمَلُوهُ إِلَی مِصْرَ لِیَمْتَارُوا بِهِ وَ کَانَ یُوسُفُ یَتَوَلَّی الْبَیْعَ بِنَفْسِهِ فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا إِخْوَتُهُ عَلَی یُوسُفَ عَرَفَهُمْ وَ لَمْ یَعْرِفُوهُ کَمَا حَکَی اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ عَلَا وَ هُمْ لَهُ مُنْکِرُونَ وَ لَمَّا جَهَّزَهُمْ بِجَهازِهِمْ وَ أَعْطَاهُمْ وَ أَحْسَنَ إِلَیْهِمْ فِی الْکَیْلِ قَالَ لَهُمْ مَنْ أَنْتُمْ قَالُوا نَحْنُ بَنُو یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِیمَ خَلِیلِ اللَّهِ الَّذِی أَلْقَاهُ نُمْرُودُ فِی النَّارِ فَلَمْ یَحْتَرِقْ وَ جَعَلَهَا اللَّهُ عَلَیْهِ بَرْداً وَ سَلاماً، قَالَ فَمَا فَعَلَ أَبُوکُمْ قَالُوا شَیْخٌ ضَعِیفٌ، قَالَ فَلَکُمْ أَخٌ غَیْرُکُمْ قَالُوا لَنَا أَخٌ مِنْ أَبِینَا لَا مِنْ أُمِّنَا، قَالَ فَإِذَا رَجَعْتُمْ إِلَیَّ فَأْتُونِی بِهِ وَ هُوَ قَوْلُهُ ائْتُونِی بِأَخٍ لَکُمْ مِنْ أَبِیکُمْ. أَ لا تَرَوْنَ أَنِّی أُوفِی الْکَیْلَ وَ أَنَا خَیْرُ الْمُنْزِلِینَ. فَإِنْ لَمْ تَأْتُونِی بِهِ فَلا کَیْلَ لَکُمْ عِنْدِی وَ لا تَقْرَبُونِ. قالُوا سَنُراوِدُ عَنْهُ أَباهُ وَ إِنَّا لَفاعِلُونَ ثُمَّ قَالَ یُوسُفُ لِقَوْمِهِ رُدُّوا هَذِهِ الْبِضَاعَهًَْ الَّتِی حَمَلُوهَا إِلَیْنَا وَ اجْعَلُوهَا فِیمَا بَیْنَ رِحَالِهِمْ حَتَّی إِذَا رَجَعُوا إِلَی مَنَازِلِهِمْ وَ رَأَوْهَا رَجَعُوا إِلَیْنَا وَ هُوَ قَوْلُهُ وَ قالَ لِفِتْیانِهِ اجْعَلُوا بِضاعَتَهُمْ فِی رِحالِهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ یَعْرِفُونَها إِذَا انْقَلَبُوا إِلی أَهْلِهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ یَرْجِعُونَ یَعْنِی کَیْ یَرْجِعُوا.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۱۰۴
entries.references: قمی، ج۱، ص۳۴۶/ البرهان/ نورالثقلین، فیه: «ولمّا و جعلها الله علیه بردا و ... کی یرجعوا» محذوف
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Ali ibn Ibrahim (may Allah have mercy on him)

Scope: (Yusuf/62)

English translation: Yusuf ordered that silos be built out of stone and lined with lime. He then ordered the crops of Egypt to be harvested; he gave every person their share and left the remainder in its stalks without threshing it, storing it in the silos. He did this for seven years. When the years of drought arrived, he would take out the stalks and sell them for whatever price he wished. The distance between him and his father was eighteen days, and they were in the desert. People from all horizons would travel to Egypt to obtain food. Ya’qub and his sons were staying in a desert where there were *muql* (doum palm) trees. Yusuf’s brothers took some of that *muql* and carried it to Egypt to trade for food. Yusuf would handle the sales himself. When his brothers entered upon Yusuf, he recognized them, but they did not recognize him, as Allah the Almighty and Exalted narrated: "And they were unaware of him." When he had provided them with their provisions, given them their share, and treated them kindly in the measuring, he said to them, "Who are you?" They replied, "We are the sons of Ya’qub ibn Ishaq ibn Ibrahim, the Friend of Allah, whom Nimrod cast into the fire, yet he did not burn, for Allah made it cool and peaceful for him." He asked, "And what has become of your father?" They said, "He is a weak, old man." He asked, "Do you have a brother other than yourselves?" They said, "We have a brother from our father, but not from our mother." He said, "When you return to me, bring him to me." This is the meaning of His saying: "Bring me a brother of yours from your father. Do you not see that I give full measure and that I am the best of hosts? But if you do not bring him to me, there shall be no measure for you with me, nor shall you come near me." They said, "We will attempt to persuade his father about him, and we will surely do it." Then Yusuf said to his people, "Return this merchandise which they brought to us and place it within their baggage, so that when they return to their homes and see it, they will return to us." This is the meaning of His saying: "And he said to his young men, 'Put their merchandise into their saddlebags so that they might recognize it when they return to their people, that perhaps they might return,'" meaning, so that they would return.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 104

References: Qumi, Vol. 1, p. 346; al-Burhan; Nur al-Thaqalayn (contains: "And when... and Allah made it cool for him... that they might return" [omitted sections]).

entries.arabic: عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِیلَ الْمِیثَمِیُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا رِبْعِیُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ لِی قَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ‌بْنُ‌أَبِی‌عَبْدِ‌اللَّهِ البصری: قُلْتُ لِأَبِی‌عَبْدِ‌اللَّهِ (علیه السلام) الْمُنْکِرُ لِهَذَا الْأَمْرِ مِنْ بَنِی‌هَاشِمٍ وَ غَیْرِهِمْ سَوَاءٌ فَقَالَ لِی لَا تَقُلِ الْمُنْکِرُ وَ لَکِنْ قُلِ الْجَاحِدُ مِنْ بَنِی‌هَاشِمٍ وَ غَیْرِهِمْ قَالَ أَبُوالْحَسَنِ فَتَفَکَّرْتُ فِیهِ فَذَکَرْتُ قَوْلَ اللَّهِ عَزَّوَجَلَّ فِی إِخْوَهًِْ یُوسُفَ فَعَرَفَهُمْ وَ هُمْ لَهُ مُنْکِرُونَ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۱۰۶
entries.references: الکافی، ج۱، ص۳۷۷
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Yusuf/ 62)

English translation: Ali ibn Isma'il al-Maythami said: Rib'i ibn Abd Allah narrated to us, saying: Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abd Allah al-Basri said to me: I said to Abu Abd Allah (peace be upon him), "Are those who deny this matter [Imamate] from the Banu Hashim and others equal?" He said to me, "Do not say 'denier' (munkir), but rather say 'rejecter' (jahid) from the Banu Hashim and others." Al-Maythami said: I reflected upon this and remembered the words of Allah, the Almighty and Majestic, regarding the brothers of Yusuf (peace be upon him): "And Yusuf recognized them, while they were to him rejecters (munkirun)."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 106

References: Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 377

entries.arabic: إنَّا وَ اللَّهِ لَا نَعُدُّ الرَّجُلَ مِنْ شِیعَتِنَا فَقِیهاً حَتَّی یُلْحَنَ لَهُ فَیَعْرِفَ اللَّحْنَ إِنَّ أَمِیرَ‌الْمُؤْمِنِینَ (علیه السلام) قَالَ عَلَی مِنْبَرِ الْکُوفَهًِْ وَ إِنَّ مِنْ وَرَائِکُمْ فِتَناً مُظْلِمَهًًْ عَمْیَاءَ مُنْکَسِفَهًًْ لَا یَنْجُو مِنْهَا إِلَّا النُّوَمَهًُْ قِیلَ یَا أَمِیرَ‌الْمُؤْمِنِینَ (علیه السلام) وَ مَا النُّوَمَهًُْ قَالَ الَّذِی یَعْرِفُ النَّاسَ وَ لَا یَعْرِفُونَهُ وَ اعْلَمُوا أَنَّ الْأَرْضَ لَاتَخْلُو مِنْ حُجَّهًٍْ لِلَّهِ وَ لَکِنَّ اللَّهَ سَیُعْمِی خَلْقَهُ مِنْهَا بِظُلْمِهِمْ وَ جَوْرِهِمْ وَ إِسْرَافِهِمْ عَلَی أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَ لَوْ خَلَتِ الْأَرْضُ سَاعَهًًْ وَاحِدَهًًْ مِنْ حُجَّهًٍْ لِلَّهِ لَسَاخَتْ بِأَهْلِهَا وَ لَکِنَّ الْحُجَّهًَْ یَعْرِفُ النَّاسَ وَ لَا یَعْرِفُونَهُ کَمَا کَانَ یُوسُفُ یَعْرِفُ النَّاسَ وَ هُمْ لَهُ مُنْکِرُونَ.
entries.persian: به خدا سوگند! کسی از شیعیان خود را فقیه نمی‌شماریم تا اینکه به رمز سخنی به او گفته می‌شود و او آن رمز را دریابد، همانا امیرالمؤمنین (علیه السلام) بر منبر کوفه فرمود: «به‌راستی که فتنه‌هایی ظلمانی و کدر و تاریک پشت سر دارید که جز نومه (کسی که عنوانی در مردم ندارد و به کلّی قدرش مجهول و ناشناخته است) کسی از آن نجات نمی‌یابد، به آن حضرت عرض شد: «ای امیرمؤمنان (علیه السلام) نومه چیست»؟ فرمود: «آن کسی است که مردم را می‌شناسد ولی مردم او را نمی‌شناسند. بدانید که زمین از حجّت خدای عزّوجلّ خالی نمی‌ماند ولی خدای عزیز به‌زودی دیده‌ی خلقش را به خاطر ظلم و ستم و زیاده روی آنان نسبت به خودشان از او نابینا می‌سازد و اگر زمین یک ساعت از حجّت خدا خالی بماند اهل خود را فرومی‌برد، ولیکن آن حجّت مردم را می‌شناسد و آنان او را نمی‌شناسند، چنان‌که یوسف (علیه السلام) مردم را [برادرانش را] می‌شناخت ولی آن‌ها او را نشناختند».
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۱۰۶
entries.references: بحارالأنوار، ج۵۱، ص۱۱۲
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Yusuf/ 62)

English translation: By Allah, we do not consider a man from our Shia to be a jurist (faqih) until he is spoken to in riddles and he understands the riddle. Verily, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said from the pulpit of Kufa: "Indeed, behind you are dark, blind, and eclipsed tribulations from which none shall be saved except the 'nawmah'." It was said, "O Commander of the Faithful, what is the 'nawmah'?" He replied, "The one who knows the people, but the people do not know him. And know that the earth is never devoid of a proof (Hujjah) for Allah, but Allah will soon blind His creation to him because of their oppression, tyranny, and excess against their own souls. If the earth were to be devoid of a proof for Allah for even a single hour, it would swallow its inhabitants. However, the proof knows the people while they do not know him, just as Yusuf (peace be upon him) knew the people [his brothers], but they were oblivious to him."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 106

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 51, p. 112

verses.arabic: فَلَمَّا رَجَعُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَبِيهِمْ قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَبَانَا مُنِعَ مِنَّا ٱلْكَيْلُ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعَنَآ أَخَانَا نَكْتَلْ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ لَحَـٰفِظُونَ
verses.english: So when they returned to their father, they said, ‘Father, the measure has been withheld from us, so let our brother go with us so that we may obtain the measure, and we will indeed take [good] care of him.’
verses.transliteration_html: Falamma rajaAAoo ila abeehim qaloo ya abana muniAAa minna alkaylu faarsil maAAana akhana naktal wainna lahu lahafithoona
verses.transliteration_text: Falamma rajaAAoo ila abeehim qaloo ya abana muniAAa minna alkaylu faarsil maAAana akhana naktal wainna lahu lahafithoona

commentary.plain_text: No commentary available for this verse

verses.arabic: قَالَ هَلْ ءَامَنُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ إِلَّا كَمَآ أَمِنتُكُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَخِيهِ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ فَٱللَّهُ خَيْرٌ حَـٰفِظًۭا ۖ وَهُوَ أَرْحَمُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ
verses.english: He said, ‘Should I not trust you with him just as I trusted you with his brother before? Yet Allah is the best of protectors, and He is the most merciful of merciful ones.’
verses.transliteration_html: Qala hal amanukum AAalayhi illa kama amintukum AAala akheehi min qablu faAllahu khayrun hafithan wahuwa arhamu alrrahimeena
verses.transliteration_text: Qala hal amanukum AAalayhi illa kama amintukum AAala akheehi min qablu faAllahu khayrun hafithan wahuwa arhamu alrrahimeena

entries.arabic: لَمَّا فَقَدَ یَعْقُوبُ (علیه السلام) یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) اشْتَدَّ حُزْنُهُ عَلَیْهِ وَ بُکَاؤُهُ حَتَّی ابْیَضَّتْ عَیْناهُ مِنَ الْحُزْنِ وَ احْتَاجَ حَاجَهًًْ شَدِیدَهًًْ وَ تَغَیَّرَتْ حَالُهُ قَالَ وَ کَانَ یَمْتَارُ الْقَمْحَ مِنْ مِصْرَ لِعِیَالِهِ فِی السَّنَهًِْ مَرَّتَیْنِ لِلشِّتَاءِ وَ الصَّیْفِ وَ إِنَّهُ بَعَثَ عِدَّهًًْ مِنْ وُلْدِهِ بِبِضَاعَهًٍْ یَسِیرَهًٍْ إِلَی مِصْرَ مَعَ رِفْقَهًٍْ خَرَجَتْ فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا عَلَی یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) وَ ذَلِکَ بَعْدَ مَا وَلَّاهُ الْعَزِیزُ مِصْرَ فَعَرَفَهُمْ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) وَ لَمْ یَعْرِفْهُ إِخْوَتُهُ لِهَیْبَهًِْ الْمَلِکِ وَ عِزِّهِ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ هَلُمُّوا بِضَاعَتَکُمْ قَبْلَ الرِّفَاقِ وَ قَالَ لِفِتْیَانِهِ عَجِّلُوا لِهَؤُلَاءِ الْکَیْلَ وَ أَوْفُوهُمْ فَإِذَا فَرَغْتُمْ فَاجْعَلُوا بِضَاعَتَهُمْ هَذِهِ فِی رِحَالِهِمْ وَ لَا تُعْلِمُوهُمْ بِذَلِکَ فَفَعَلُوا ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُمْ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) قَدْ بَلَغَنِی أَنَّهُ کَانَ لَکُمْ أَخَوَانِ لِأَبِیکُمْ فَمَا فَعَلَا قَالُوا أَمَّا الْکَبِیرُ مِنْهُمَا فَإِنَّ الذِّئْبَ أَکَلَهُ وَ أَمَّا الصَّغِیرُ فَخَلَّفْنَاهُ عِنْدَ أَبِیهِ وَ هُوَ بِهِ ضَنِینٌ وَ عَلَیْهِ شَفِیقٌ قَالَ فَإِنِّی أُحِبُّ أَنْ تَأْتُونِی بِهِ مَعَکُمْ إِذَا جِئْتُمْ لِتَمْتَارُوا فَإِنْ لَمْ تَأْتُونِی بِهِ فَلا کَیْلَ لَکُمْ عِنْدِی وَ لا تَقْرَبُونِ قالُوا سَنُراوِدُ عَنْهُ أَباهُ وَ إِنَّا لَفاعِلُونَ* فَلَمَّا رَجَعُوا إِلی أَبِیهِمْ ... فَتَحُوا مَتاعَهُمْ فَوَجَدُوا بِضَاعَتَهُمْ فِیهِ قالُوا یا أَبانا ما نَبْغِی هذِهِ بِضاعَتُنا قَدْ رُدَّتْ إِلَیْنا وَ کِیلَ لَنَا کَیْلٌ قَدْ زَادَ حِمْلَ بَعِیرٍ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعَنا أَخانا نَکْتَلْ وَ إِنَّا لَهُ لَحافِظُونَ قالَ هَلْ آمَنُکُمْ عَلَیْهِ إِلَّا کَما أَمِنْتُکُمْ عَلی أَخِیهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ فَلَمَّا احْتَاجُوا إِلَی الْمِیرَهًِْ بَعْدَ سِتَّهًِْ أَشْهُرٍ بَعَثَهُمْ یَعْقُوبُ (علیه السلام) وَ بَعَثَ مَعَهُمْ بِضَاعَهًًْ یَسِیرَهًًْ وَ بَعَثَ مَعَهُمُ ابْنَ یَامِیلَ{یَامِینَ} وَ أَخَذَ عَلَیْهِمْ بِذَلِکَ مَوْثِقاً مِنَ اللَّهِ لَتَأْتُنَّنِی بِهِ إِلَّا أَنْ یُحاطَ بِکُمْ أَجْمَعِینَ فَانْطَلَقُوا مَعَ الرِّفَاقِ حَتَّی دَخَلُوا عَلَی یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) فَقَالَ لَهُمْ مَعَکُمُ ابْنُ یَامِیلَ {یَامِینَ} قَالُوا نَعَمْ هُوَ فِی الرَّحْلِ قَالَ لَهُمْ فَأْتُوْنِی بِهِ فَأَتَوْهُ بِهِ وَ هُوَ فِی دَارِ الْمَلِکِ فَقَالَ أَدْخِلُوهُ وَحْدَهُ فَأَدْخَلُوهُ عَلَیْهِ فَضَمَّهُ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) إِلَیْهِ وَ بَکَی وَ قَالَ لَهُ أَنَا أَخُوکَ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) فَلَا تَبْتَئِسْ بِمَا تَرَانِی أَعْمَلُ وَ اکْتُمْ مَا أَخْبَرْتُکَ بِهِ وَ لَا تَحْزَنْ وَ لَا تَخَفْ ثُمَّ أَخْرَجَهُ إِلَیْهِمْ وَ أَمَرَ فِتْیَتَهُ أَنْ یَأْخُذُوا بِضَاعَتَهُمْ وَ یُعَجِّلُوا لَهُمُ الْکَیْلَ فَإِذَا فَرَغُوا جَعَلُوا الْمِکْیَالَ فِی رَحْلِ ابْنِ یَامِیلَ {یَامِینَ} فَفَعَلُوا بِهِ ذَلِکَ وَ ارْتَحَلَ الْقَوْمُ مَعَ الرِّفْقَهًِْ فَمَضَوْا فَلَحِقَهُمْ یُوسُفُ (علیه السلام) وَ فِتْیَتُهُ فَنَادَوْا فِیهِمْ أَیَّتُهَا الْعِیرُ إِنَّکُمْ لَسارِقُونَ قالُوا وَ أَقْبَلُوا عَلَیْهِمْ ما ذا تَفْقِدُونَ* قالُوا نَفْقِدُ صُواعَ الْمَلِکِ وَ لِمَنْ جاءَ بِهِ حِمْلُ بَعِیرٍ وَ أَنَا بِهِ زَعِیمٌ* قالُوا تَاللهِ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمْ ما جِئْنا لِنُفْسِدَ فِی الْأَرْضِ وَ ما کُنَّا سارِقِینَ* قالُوا فَما جَزاؤُهُ إِنْ کُنْتُمْ کاذِبِینَ* قالُوا جَزاؤُهُ مَنْ وُجِدَ فِی رَحْلِهِ فَهُوَ جَزاؤُهُ ... * قَالَ فَبَدَأَ بِأَوْعِیَتِهِمْ قَبْلَ وِعاءِ أَخِیهِ ثُمَّ اسْتَخْرَجَها مِنْ وِعاءِ أَخِیهِ ... قالُوا إِنْ یَسْرِقْ فَقَدْ سَرَقَ أَخٌ لَهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ ... فَقَالَ لَهُمْ یُوسُفُ ارْتَحِلُوا عَنْ بِلَادِنَا قالُوا یا أَیُّهَا الْعَزِیزُ إِنَّ لَهُ أَباً شَیْخاً کَبِیراً وَ قَدْ أَخَذَ عَلَیْنَا مَوْثِقاً مِنَ اللهِ لِنَرُدَّ بِهِ إِلَیْهِ فَخُذْ أَحَدَنا مَکانَهُ إِنَّا نَراکَ مِنَ الْمُحْسِنِینَ* إِنْ فَعَلْتَ قالَ مَعاذَ اللهِ أَنْ نَأْخُذَ إِلَّا مَنْ وَجَدْنا مَتاعَنا عِنْدَهُ ... فَقالَ کَبِیرُهُمْ إِنِّی لَسْتُ أَبْرَحُ الْأَرْضَ حَتَّی یَأْذَنَ لِی أَبِی أَوْ یَحْکُمَ اللَّهُ لِی وَ مَضَی إِخْوَهًُْ یُوسُفَ (علیه السلام) حَتَّی دَخَلُوا عَلَی یَعْقُوبَ (علیه السلام) فَقَالَ لَهُمْ فَأَیْنَ ابْنُ یَامِیلَ{یَامِینَ} قَالُوا ابْنُ یَامِیلَ {یَامِینَ} سَرَقَ مِکْیَالَ الْمَلِکِ فَأَخَذَ الْمَلِکُ سَرِقَتَهُ فَحَبَسَ عِنْدَهُ فَاسْأَلْ أَهْلَ الْقَرْیَهًِْ وَ الْعِیْرَ حَتَّی یُخْبِرُوکَ بِذَلِکَ فَاسْتَرْجَعَ وَ اسْتَعْبَرَ وَ اشْتَدَّ حُزْنُهُ حَتَّی تَقَوَّسَ ظَهْرُهُ.

entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Yusuf/ 64)

English translation: When Ya‘qub (peace be upon him) lost Yusuf (peace be upon him), his grief and weeping for him intensified until his eyes turned white from sorrow. He fell into severe need, and his condition changed. It is said that he would travel to Egypt to obtain wheat for his family twice a year, once in the winter and once in the summer. He sent a number of his sons to Egypt with a small amount of capital along with a departing caravan. When they entered upon Yusuf (peace be upon him)—this being after the Aziz had appointed him over Egypt—Yusuf (peace be upon him) recognized them, but his brothers did not recognize him due to the majesty and dignity of the king. He said to them, "Bring your capital before the others," and he said to his servants, "Hasten the measuring for these people and give them full measure. When you have finished, place their capital in their saddlebags without them knowing." They did so. Then Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to them, "It has reached me that you had two brothers from your father; what became of them?" They replied, "As for the elder of the two, the wolf ate him, and as for the younger, we left him with his father, who is attached to him and deeply affectionate toward him." He said, "I would like you to bring him to me with you when you come to obtain provisions. If you do not bring him to me, you shall have no measure from me, nor shall you approach me." They said, "We will attempt to persuade his father about him, and we will indeed do it." When they returned to their father... they opened their baggage and found their capital within it. They said, "O our father, what more could we desire? This is our capital returned to us, and we have been given a measure that exceeds a camel’s load. So send our brother with us so we may obtain a measure, and we will indeed be his guardians." He said, "Shall I entrust him to you except as I entrusted you with his brother before?" When they were in need of provisions six months later, Ya‘qub (peace be upon him) sent them with a small amount of capital and sent with them Ibn Yamin (Benjamin). He took a solemn oath from them by Allah that they would bring him back to him, unless they were completely surrounded. They set out with the caravan until they entered upon Yusuf (peace be upon him). He said to them, "Is Ibn Yamin with you?" They said, "Yes, he is in the caravan." He said, "Bring him to me." They brought him to him while he was in the king’s palace. He said, "Bring him in alone." They brought him in to him, and Yusuf (peace be upon him) embraced him, wept, and said to him, "I am your brother Yusuf (peace be upon him). Do not be distressed by what you see me doing, and conceal what I have told you. Do not grieve, and do not fear." Then he brought him out to them and ordered his servants to take their capital and hasten their measure, and when they finished, to place the measuring cup in the saddlebag of Ibn Yamin. They did so. The group departed with the caravan, and as they went, Yusuf (peace be upon him) and his servants caught up with them and called out to them, "O caravan, you are indeed thieves!" They turned to them and said, "What is it that you have lost?" They said, "We have lost the king’s measuring cup, and whoever brings it shall have a camel’s load, and I am a guarantor for it." They said, "By Allah, you know we did not come to cause corruption in the land, and we were not thieves." They said, "What is its penalty if you are liars?" They said, "Its penalty is that whoever is found in his saddlebag, he is the penalty..." He began with their bags before his brother’s bag, then he extracted it from his brother’s bag... They said, "If he has stolen, a brother of his had stolen before..." Yusuf (peace be upon him) said to them, "Depart from our lands." They said, "O Aziz, he has a very old father, and he has taken a solemn oath from us by Allah to return him to him. So take one of us in his place; we see that you are of the doers of good." He said, "I seek refuge in Allah from taking anyone except him with whom we found our property..." The eldest of them said, "I will not leave this land until my father permits me or Allah decides for me." The brothers of Yusuf (peace be upon him) went on until they entered upon Ya‘qub (peace be upon him). He said to them, "Where is Ibn Yamin?" They said, "Ibn Yamin stole the king’s measuring cup, so the king took his stolen item and imprisoned him. Ask the people of the town and the caravan, and they will inform you of that." He recited the Istirja‘ (Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji‘un), wept, and his grief intensified until his back became bent.

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 106

References: Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 12, p. 12; Al-‘Ayyashi, Vol. 2, p. 181; Al-Burhan

verses.arabic: وَلَمَّا فَتَحُوا۟ مَتَـٰعَهُمْ وَجَدُوا۟ بِضَـٰعَتَهُمْ رُدَّتْ إِلَيْهِمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَبَانَا مَا نَبْغِى ۖ هَـٰذِهِۦ بِضَـٰعَتُنَا رُدَّتْ إِلَيْنَا ۖ وَنَمِيرُ أَهْلَنَا وَنَحْفَظُ أَخَانَا وَنَزْدَادُ كَيْلَ بَعِيرٍۢ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ كَيْلٌۭ يَسِيرٌۭ
verses.english: And when they opened their baggage, they found their money restored to them. They said, ‘Father, what [more] do we want?! This is our money, restored to us! We will get provisions for our family and take care of our brother, and add another camel-load of rations. These are meagre rations.’
verses.transliteration_html: Walamma fatahoo mataAAahum wajadoo bidaAAatahum ruddat ilayhim qaloo ya abana ma nabghee hathihi bidaAAatuna ruddat ilayna wanameeru ahlana wanahfathu akhana wanazdadu kayla baAAeerin thalika kaylun yaseerun
verses.transliteration_text: Walamma fatahoo mataAAahum wajadoo bidaAAatahum ruddat ilayhim qaloo ya abana ma nabghee hathihi bidaAAatuna ruddat ilayna wanameeru ahlana wanahfathu akhana wanazdadu kayla baAAeerin thalika kaylun yaseerun

commentary.plain_text:
۱

(یوسف/ ۶۵)

الکاظم (علیه السلام)-

عَنْ أَحْمَدَ‌بْنِ‌عُمَرَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَاالْحَسَنِ (علیه السلام) لِمَ سُمِّیَ أَمِیرَ‌الْمُؤْمِنِینَ (علیه السلام) قَالَ لِأَنَّهُ یَمِیرُهُمْ الْعِلْمَ أَمَا سَمِعْتَ فِی کِتَابِ اللَّهِ وَ نَمِیرُ أَهْلَنا.

امام کاظم (علیه السلام)-

احمدبن‌عمر گوید: از امام کاظم (علیه السلام) پرسیدم: «فدایتان شوم! چرا امیرمؤمنان (علیه السلام)، امیرمؤمنان نامیده شد»؟ فرمود: «چون آذوقه‌ای که امام (علیه السلام) به آنان می‌دهد، دانش است؛ مگر این سخن خداوند نَمِیرُ أَهْلَنَا را نشنیده‌ای»؟!

تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۱۱۰

الکافی، ج۱، ص۴۱۲/ نورالثقلین/ بحارالأنوار، ج۳۷، ص۲۹۳/ العیاشی، ج۲، ص۱۸۴/ علل الشرایع، ج۱، ص۱۶۱/ معانی الأخبار، ص۶۳/ البرهان

entries.arabic: عَنْ أَحْمَدَ‌بْنِ‌عُمَرَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَاالْحَسَنِ (علیه السلام) لِمَ سُمِّیَ أَمِیرَ‌الْمُؤْمِنِینَ (علیه السلام) قَالَ لِأَنَّهُ یَمِیرُهُمْ الْعِلْمَ أَمَا سَمِعْتَ فِی کِتَابِ اللَّهِ وَ نَمِیرُ أَهْلَنا.

entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him)

Scope: (Yusuf/ 65)

English translation: Ahmad ibn Umar said: I asked Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him), "Why was he named Amir al-Mu'minin (Commander of the Faithful)?" He replied, "Because he provides them with knowledge as sustenance (yamiru-hum). Have you not heard in the Book of Allah: 'And we will provide for our family' (wa namiru ahlana)?"

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 110

References: al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 412; Nur al-Thaqalayn; Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 37, p. 293; al-Ayyashi, Vol. 2, p. 184; 'Ilal al-Shara'i', Vol. 1, p. 161; Ma'ani al-Akhbar, p. 63; al-Burhan

verses.arabic: قَالَ لَنْ أُرْسِلَهُۥ مَعَكُمْ حَتَّىٰ تُؤْتُونِ مَوْثِقًۭا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ لَتَأْتُنَّنِى بِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَن يُحَاطَ بِكُمْ ۖ فَلَمَّآ ءَاتَوْهُ مَوْثِقَهُمْ قَالَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مَا نَقُولُ وَكِيلٌۭ
verses.english: He said, ‘I will not let him go with you until you give me a [solemn] pledge by Allah that you will surely bring him back to me, unless you are made to perish.’ When they had given him their [solemn] pledge, he said, ‘Allah is witness over what we say.’
verses.transliteration_html: Qala lan orsilahu maAAakum hatta tutooni mawthiqan mina Allahi latatunnanee bihi illa an yuhata bikum falamma atawhu mawthiqahum qala Allahu AAala ma naqoolu wakeelun
verses.transliteration_text: Qala lan orsilahu maAAakum hatta tutooni mawthiqan mina Allahi latatunnanee bihi illa an yuhata bikum falamma atawhu mawthiqahum qala Allahu AAala ma naqoolu wakeelun

commentary.plain_text:
۱

(یوسف/ ۶۶)

ابن‌عبّاس (رحمة الله علیه)-

سُئِلَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ (رحمة الله علیه) عَنِ الْمَوْثِقِ الَّذِی أَخَذَهُ یَعْقُوبُ عَلَی أَوْلَادِهِ؟ فَقَالَ: قَالَ لَهُمْ: مَعْشَرَ أَوْلَادِی، إِنْ جِئْتُمُونِی بِوَلَدِی وَ إِلَّا فَأَنْتُمْ بُرَآءُ مِنَ النَّبِیِّ الْأُمِّیِّ الَّذِی یَکُونُ فِی آخِرِ الزَّمَانِ، لَهُ أُمَّةٌ یَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَ بِهِ یَعْدِلُونَ، أَهْلُ کَلِمَهًٍْ عَظِیمَهًٍْ، أَعْظَمُ مِنَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَ الْأَرْضِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، عَلِیٌّ وَلِیُّ اللَّهِ، صَاحِبُ النَّاقَهًِْ وَ الْقَضِیبِ الَّذِی سَمَّاهُ اللَّهُ حَبِیباً، ذُوالْوَجْهِ الْأَقْمَرِ، وَ الْجَبِینِ الْأَزْهَرِ، وَ الْحَوْضِ وَ الْکَوْثَرِ، وَ الْمَقَامِ الْمَشْهُودِ، لَهُ ابْنُ عَمٍّ یُسَمَّی حَیْدَرَهًًْ، زَوْجُ ابْنَتِهِ، وَ خَلِیفَتُهُ عَلَی قَوْمِهِ، عَلِیُّ بْنُ أَبِی طَالِبٍ (علیه السلام)، تَأْتُونَهُ وَ هُوَ مُعْرِضٌ عَنْکُمْ بِوَجْهِهِ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهًِْ إِنْ خُنْتُمُونِی فِی وَلَدِی. قَالُوا: نَعَمْ. قَالَ یَعْقُوبُ (علیه السلام): فَاللهُ خَیْرٌ حَافِظاً وَ هُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِینَ. قَالُوا: نَعَمْ؛ فَاللهُ خَیْرٌ حافِظاً وَ هُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِینَ.

ابن‌عبّاس (رحمة الله علیه)-

از ابن‌عبّاس (رحمة الله علیه) در مورد پیمانی که یعقوب (علیه السلام) از فرزندانش گرفت سؤال شد. در جواب فرمود: «یعقوب (علیه السلام) به آن‌ها گفت: «ای فرزندانم! باید فرزندم [بنیامین] را بازگردانید در غیر این‌صورت شما از پیامبر اُمّی [از صفات پیامبر اسلام (صلی الله علیه و آله)] که در آخرالزّمان مبعوث خواهد شد بیزارید، او پیروانی دارد که به‌سوی حق هدایت می‌کنند و به حق و عدالت حکم می‌نمایند، او صاحب کلام با عظمتی است که عظمتش از آسمان‌ها و زمین بیشتر می‌باشد؛ [یعنی] لا إله إلا الله محمّد رسول الله علی ولی الله او صاحب شتر ماده [شتری که پیامبر اسلام (صلی الله علیه و آله) بر آن سوار می‌شدند و نامش «عضباء» بوده] و «قضیب» [نام یکی از شمشیرهای پیامبر اسلام (صلی الله علیه و آله)] است و خداوند او را حبیبِ خود نامیده است. او صاحب چهره‌ای همچون ماه و پیشانی درخشان و صاحب حوض و کوثر و مقام مشهود [اشاره به شهود کامل پیامبر (صلی الله علیه و آله) در شب معراج] است؛ پسر عمویی دارد که به او «حیدر» گویند و شوهر دختر او و جانشین او میان امّتش می‌باشد و نامش علیّ‌بن‌ابی‌طالب (علیه السلام) است. اگر شما در مورد فرزندم [بنیامین] به من خیانت کنید [و او را بازنگردانید] در روز قیامت درحالی با او روبرو خواهید شد که او از شما روی بر می‌گرداند. برادران گفتند: «آری! [چنین پیمانی می‌بندیم]». آنگاه یعقوب (علیه السلام) گفت: و [در هر حال]، خداوند بهترین حافظ، و مهربان‌ترین مهربانان است. (

یوسف/۶۴

) آن‌ها نیز گفتند: «آری! و [در هر حال]، خداوند بهترین حافظ، و مهربان‌ترین مهربانان است. (

یوسف/۶۴

)».

تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۱۱۲

البرهان

entries.arabic: سُئِلَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ (رحمة الله علیه) عَنِ الْمَوْثِقِ الَّذِی أَخَذَهُ یَعْقُوبُ عَلَی أَوْلَادِهِ؟ فَقَالَ: قَالَ لَهُمْ: مَعْشَرَ أَوْلَادِی، إِنْ جِئْتُمُونِی بِوَلَدِی وَ إِلَّا فَأَنْتُمْ بُرَآءُ مِنَ النَّبِیِّ الْأُمِّیِّ الَّذِی یَکُونُ فِی آخِرِ الزَّمَانِ، لَهُ أُمَّةٌ یَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَ بِهِ یَعْدِلُونَ، أَهْلُ کَلِمَهًٍْ عَظِیمَهًٍْ، أَعْظَمُ مِنَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَ الْأَرْضِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، عَلِیٌّ وَلِیُّ اللَّهِ، صَاحِبُ النَّاقَهًِْ وَ الْقَضِیبِ الَّذِی سَمَّاهُ اللَّهُ حَبِیباً، ذُوالْوَجْهِ الْأَقْمَرِ، وَ الْجَبِینِ الْأَزْهَرِ، وَ الْحَوْضِ وَ الْکَوْثَرِ، وَ الْمَقَامِ الْمَشْهُودِ، لَهُ ابْنُ عَمٍّ یُسَمَّی حَیْدَرَهًًْ، زَوْجُ ابْنَتِهِ، وَ خَلِیفَتُهُ عَلَی قَوْمِهِ، عَلِیُّ بْنُ أَبِی طَالِبٍ (علیه السلام)، تَأْتُونَهُ وَ هُوَ مُعْرِضٌ عَنْکُمْ بِوَجْهِهِ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهًِْ إِنْ خُنْتُمُونِی فِی وَلَدِی. قَالُوا: نَعَمْ. قَالَ یَعْقُوبُ (علیه السلام): فَاللهُ خَیْرٌ حَافِظاً وَ هُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِینَ. قَالُوا: نَعَمْ؛ فَاللهُ خَیْرٌ حافِظاً وَ هُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِینَ.
entries.book: تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۱۱۲
entries.references: البرهان
entries.english_translation:
Attribution: Ibn Abbas (may Allah have mercy on him)

Scope: (Yusuf/66)

English translation: Ibn Abbas (may Allah have mercy on him) was asked about the solemn pledge that Jacob (peace be upon him) took from his sons. He replied: He said to them, "O my sons, if you do not bring my son back to me, then you are disassociated from the Unlettered Prophet who will appear in the end times. He will have a nation that guides by the truth and judges by it. He is the possessor of a great word, greater than the heavens and the earth: 'There is no god but Allah, Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, Ali is the Wali (friend/representative) of Allah.' He is the possessor of the she-camel and the staff (Qadib), whom Allah has named His Beloved. He is the one with the moon-like face, the radiant forehead, the Basin (Hawd), the Abundance (Kawthar), and the Witnessed Station. He has a cousin named Haydarah, the husband of his daughter and his successor over his people, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). If you betray me regarding my son, you will come to him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will turn his face away from you." They said, "Yes." Jacob (peace be upon him) said, "So Allah is the best guardian, and He is the Most Merciful of the merciful." They said, "Yes; so Allah is the best guardian, and He is the Most Merciful of the merciful."

Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 112

References: Al-Burhan

verses.arabic: وَقَالَ يَـٰبَنِىَّ لَا تَدْخُلُوا۟ مِنۢ بَابٍۢ وَٰحِدٍۢ وَٱدْخُلُوا۟ مِنْ أَبْوَٰبٍۢ مُّتَفَرِّقَةٍۢ ۖ وَمَآ أُغْنِى عَنكُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍ ۖ إِنِ ٱلْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ ۖ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ۖ وَعَلَيْهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُتَوَكِّلُونَ
verses.english: And he said, ‘My sons, do not enter by one gate, but enter by separate gates, though I cannot avail you anything against Allah. Sovereignty belongs only to Allah. In Him I have put my trust; and in Him let all the trusting put their trust.’
verses.transliteration_html: Waqala ya baniyya la tadkhuloo min babin wahidin waodkhuloo min abwabin mutafarriqatin wama oghnee AAankum mina Allahi min shayin ini alhukmu illa lillahi AAalayhi tawakkaltu waAAalayhi falyatawakkali almutawakkiloona
verses.transliteration_text: Waqala ya baniyya la tadkhuloo min babin wahidin waodkhuloo min abwabin mutafarriqatin wama oghnee AAankum mina Allahi min shayin ini alhukmu illa lillahi AAalayhi tawakkaltu waAAalayhi falyatawakkali almutawakkiloona

commentary.plain_text:
۱

(یوسف/ ۶۷)

الرّسول (صلی الله علیه و آله)-

إِنَّ الْعَیْنَ حَقٌّ وَ الْعَیْنُ تَسْتَنْزِلُ الْحَالِقَ.

پیامبر (صلی الله علیه و آله)-

چشم زخم درست است تا آنجا که قلّه‌ی کوه را فرو آورد.

تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۱۱۲

نورالثقلین

۲

(یوسف/ ۶۷)

الصّادق (علیه السلام)-

عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِی‌عَبْدِ‌اللَّهِ (علیه السلام): فِی قَولِ اللهِ عَزَّوَجَلَّ وَ عَلَی اللهِ فَلْیَتَوَکَّلِ الْمُتَوَکِّلُونَ، قَالَ الزَّارِعُونَ.

امام صادق (علیه السلام)-

وَ علَی الله فَلْیَتَوَکَّلِ المُؤْمِنُونَ؛ مقصود، کشاورزان هستند.

تفسیر اهل بیت علیهم السلام ج۷، ص۱۱۲

وسایل الشیعهًْ، ج۱۷، ص۴۲/ البرهان

entries.arabic: إِنَّ الْعَیْنَ حَقٌّ وَ الْعَیْنُ تَسْتَنْزِلُ الْحَالِقَ.

Attribution: The Messenger (peace be upon him and his family)
Scope: (Yusuf/ 67)
English translation: The evil eye is real, and the evil eye can bring down the mountain peak.
Book: Tafsir Ahl al-Bayt (peace be upon them), Vol. 7, p. 112
References: Nur al-Thaqalayn
